channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: Senior churn stifling hospital group, warns leading CEO
item.description: A trust “alliance” that includes England’s top-rated general acute provider has been stifled by leadership churn and a shift in national policy, but will now develop an ambitious joint clinical strategy, it has revealed.

item.title: Regulators demand answers on advice and guidance
item.description: Answers are needed from NHS England and others on 11 issues to make sure its controversial expansion of advice and guidance is safe, the Care Quality Commission has declared.

item.title: Trust announces ‘exceptional’ leader as new chair
item.description: A Yorkshire trust has appointed an experienced healthcare leader as its new chair.

item.title: Regulator ‘missing many AI incidents’
item.description: Safety concerns linked to AI voice tech are not being properly reported because many providers are unaware of the regulation system or too busy to use it, experts have told HSJ.

item.title: Real-terms CEO pay fell 13% in five years
item.description: Trust CEO pay has dropped by more than a tenth in real terms in five years, government advisers have said.

item.title: 21 trusts ‘delivered year’s elective recovery in single month’
item.description: Twenty-one trusts delivered their entire 2025-26 elective improvement in March alone, analysis shows, prompting concerns about the “fragility and sustainability” of the NHS’s waiting list recovery.

item.title: Senior managers to get ‘well deserved’ pay rise
item.description: Senior NHS managers will get a 3 per cent pay rise for 2026-27, the government has announced.

item.title: National role for retired trust CEO
item.description: A recently retired acute trust chief executive is returning to a national role just months after ending a 40-year career in the NHS.

item.title: Trust CEO quits despite being cleared of misconduct
item.description: A trust chief executive who was cleared of misconduct after being wrongly suspended by her chair has resigned.

item.title: AI firm sues government over procurement decision
item.description: An AI scribing firm that also runs one of primary care’s most widely used GP triage platforms has filed a court claim against the government over a procurement decision.

item.title: Growing private services creating ‘regulatory gap’, claims watchdog
item.description: There is a growing “regulatory gap” around several NHS services where private provision has grown rapidly, the Parliamentary watchdog has told HSJ.

item.title: Exclusive: CQC demands answers over Southport attack data breach
item.description: The Care Quality Commission is investigating whether the trust where staff inappropriately viewed the records of Southport attack victims met its “duty of candour” after the provider was accused of a “cover up”, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: NHS-owned consultancy faces closure
item.description: An NHS-owned consultancy is facing closure after reporting significant financial losses, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: Staff dismissed for viewing Nottingham attack victims’ files
item.description: Eleven staff have been dismissed for inappropriately viewing the medical records of Nottingham stabbing victims without a clinical reason, a trust has revealed.

item.title: Smaller tech firms face NHS App ‘guillotine’
item.description: NHS England is planning to bring online consultation functionality in-house through the NHS App, potentially dismantling a market worth up to £32m a year, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: Trust leadership gets red rating after boardroom row
item.description: A trust whose chair quit after a boardroom row has become the first to have its “capability” rating downgraded by NHS England.

item.title: CQC warns Health Bill could leave it fighting itself in court
item.description: The Care Quality Commission has warned that government plans for it to absorb the national patient safety investigations body could leave it arguing against itself in the High Court.

item.title: Neighbourhood plans ‘in danger’, says top five trust leader
item.description: The government’s neighbourhood health agenda is “in danger of not happening” amid a lack of clarity over governance structures and funding, the chair of England’s fourth-largest trust has claimed.

item.title: Dozens of stroke units lose ‘A’ ratings
item.description: Close to 80 per cent of stroke units are falling well short against a swathe of new standards introduced to the high-profile national audit, according to HSJ analysis.

item.title: CEO returns to English trust after four years in Wales
item.description: The CEO of a Welsh health board is taking over a large mental health and community trust.
